name: Copilot MCP
what_it_does: VS Code extension that provides a unified interface for discovering, installing, and managing MCP servers and agent skills for GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Codex CLI. Includes a skills registry (skills.sh) and optional Cloud MCP remote deployment.
use_cases: Managing multiple MCP server connections from within VS Code
Discovering and installing MCP skills from the skills.sh registry
Connecting MCP tools to GitHub Copilot Chat
Running MCP servers remotely via Cloud MCP without local setup
not_for: Standalone MCP server hosting outside VS Code
best_when: You use VS Code with GitHub Copilot and want a GUI for managing MCP servers and discovering new skills without manual config editing.
avoid_when: You don't use VS Code or prefer CLI-based MCP configuration. Also avoid if you need fine-grained control over MCP server settings.

alternatives.name: Claude Code CLI
alternatives.note: Built-in MCP management via `claude mcp add`

alternatives.name: Cursor
alternatives.note: Has native MCP support built into the editor

alternatives.name: mcp-installer
alternatives.note: CLI tool for installing MCP servers

auth.notes: Requires GitHub Copilot Chat extension. Cloud MCP remote option uses OAuth authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Extension is free; requires paid GitHub Copilot subscription for Copilot features. Cloud MCP may have separate pricing.
pricing.notes: GPL-3.0 licensed. GitHub Copilot subscription required for full functionality.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No documentation on error handling or error messages from MCP tools.
known_agent_gotchas: VS Code-only — not usable outside the editor
Depends on GitHub Copilot Chat extension being installed and authenticated
GPL-3.0 license may be restrictive for some use cases
Includes vendored code from anomalyco/opencode — provenance unclear
